prompt: Can you help me convert microsoft/Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct to run on my Intel NPU? I want to use winml or whatever the windows ml toolkit is.

assertions.text: Response identifies Phi-3 as a decoder-only LLM / generative model (or equivalently flags it as the kind of model ModelKit doesn't support).
assertions.rationale: Architectural classification is the trigger for the out-of-scope refusal.

assertions.text: Response clearly states that this model is NOT supported / out of scope for the winml ModelKit pipeline today.
assertions.rationale: The skill's hard rule — stop and say so when handed an LLM. A hedged "it might work" or "give it a try" is a failure.

assertions.text: Response does NOT walk the user through export / optimize / quantize / compile / build commands as if Phi-3 will go through the pipeline.
assertions.rationale: Skill says the pipeline will fail mid-way with non-obvious errors — don't pretend it'll work.

assertions.text: Response either mentions that LLM support is on the roadmap (late 2026) OR suggests a legitimate alternative path for running Phi-3 on Intel NPU (OpenVINO GenAI, ONNX Runtime GenAI, etc.).
assertions.rationale: Don't just say no — give the user somewhere useful to go next.
